text: A normal explicit Fabric Login response is an LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI and the Common Service N_Port/F_Port bit set to one, indicating an Fx_Port.

evidence_refs.quote: a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI, and the Common Ser-
vice N_Port/F_Port bit set to one (Fx_Port) - This is the normal response to a Fabric Login re-

text: For the normal Fabric LS_ACC response, the D_ID of the LS_ACC shall be the N_Port_ID assigned by the Fabric.

evidence_refs.quote: quest.  The D_ID of the LS_ACC shall be the N_Port_ID assigned by the Fabric.

text: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was 000000h, then in the normal Fabric LS_ACC response the D_ID shall be XXXXXX.

evidence_refs.quote: the FLOGI was 000000h, the D_ID shall be XXXXXX.

text: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was 0000h || YY, then in the normal Fabric LS_ACC response the D_ID shall be XXXXYY.

evidence_refs.quote: YY, the D_ID shall be XXXXYY.

text: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was XXXXXX, then in the normal Fabric LS_ACC response the D_ID shall be the same value of XXXXXX.

evidence_refs.quote: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was XXXXXX, the D_ID shall be
same value of XXXXXX.

text: The normal Fabric LS_ACC payload shall include the Service Parameters for the entire Fabric, a 64-bit F_Port_Name, and a 64-bit Fabric_Name.

evidence_refs.quote: same value of XXXXXX.  The Payload shall include the Service Parameters for the entire Fab-
ric, a 64-bit F_Port_Name and 64-bit Fabric_Name.

text: After the normal Fabric LS_ACC response, the Nx_Port may continue operation with other Nx_Ports if the N_Port_ID, F_Port_Name, and Fabric_Name are the same as in a previous Fabric Login, or proceed to N_Port Login.

evidence_refs.quote: tables 150 and 155.  The Nx_Port may continue operation with other Nx_Ports if the
N_Port_ID, F_Port_Name, and Fabric_Name are the same as in a previous Fabric Login or
proceed to N_Port Login.

text: Another possible FLOGI response is an LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI and the Common Service Nx_Port/F_Port bit set to zero, indicating a point-to-point connection with another Nx_Port.

evidence_refs.quote: b) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI, and the Common Ser-
vice Nx_Port/F_Port bit set to zero (Nx_Port) This indicates a point-to-point connection with an-

text: For the point-to-point LS_ACC response from another Nx_Port, the D_ID of the LS_ACC shall be the S_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: other Nx_Port.  The D_ID of the LS_ACC shall be the S_ID of the FLOGI.

text: For the point-to-point LS_ACC response from another Nx_Port, the payload shall include the Service Parameters from the FLOGI with all classes marked invalid, a 64-bit N_Port_Name, and a 64-bit Node_Name of the connected Nx_Port.

evidence_refs.quote: include the Service Parameters from the FLOGI with all classes marked invalid, a 64-bit
N_Port_Name and 64-bit Node_Name of the connected Nx_Port.

text: For the point-to-point LS_ACC response from another Nx_Port, if the received N_Port_Name is less than its N_Port_Name, the Nx_Port proceeds to N_Port Login.

evidence_refs.quote: N_Port_Name is less than its N_Port_Name, the Nx_Port proceeds to N_Port Login.

text: For the point-to-point LS_ACC response from another Nx_Port, if the received N_Port_Name is greater than its N_Port_Name, the Nx_Port waits for PLOGI from the attached N_Port.

evidence_refs.quote: ceived N_Port_Name is greater than its N_Port_Name, the Nx_Port waits for PLOGI from the
attached N_Port.

text: A possible FLOGI response is F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

text: For the F_BSY response to FLOGI, the D_ID shall be the S_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: The D_ID shall be the S_ID of the FLO-
GI.

text: For the F_BSY response to FLOGI, the Fabric is busy and the Nx_Port may retry the FLOGI again later.

evidence_refs.quote: GI.  The Fabric is busy.  The Nx_Port may retry the FLOGI again later.

text: A possible FLOGI response is a P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: d) P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

text: For the P_BSY response to FLOGI, the D_ID shall be the S_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: The D_ID shall be the S_ID
of the FLOGI.

text: For the P_BSY response to FLOGI, this indicates a point-to-point connection with another Nx_Port that is currently busy, and the Nx_Port may proceed to N_Port Login after a delay to allow the destination Nx_Port to become not busy.

evidence_refs.quote: of the FLOGI.  This indicates a point-to-point connection with another Nx_Port that is currently
busy.  The Nx_Port may proceed to N_Port Login after a delay to allow the destination Nx_Port
to become not busy.

text: A possible FLOGI response is an F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: e) F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

text: For the F_RJT response to FLOGI, the D_ID shall be the S_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: The D_ID shall be the S_ID of
the FLOGI.

text: For the F_RJT response to FLOGI, if the reason code is "Class not supported", the Nx_Port may originate a FLOGI in a different class.

evidence_refs.quote: the FLOGI.  The Fabric has rejected the FLOGI request.  The reason code contained in the
Payload determines the Nx_Port’s action.  If the reason code is "Class not supported", the
Nx_Port may originate a FLOGI in a different class.

text: For the F_RJT response to FLOGI, if the reason code is "Invalid S_ID" and the rejected FLOGI S_ID was 000000h or 0000h || YY, the Nx_Port may select a 24-bit value XXXXXX for its N_Port_ID by a method outside this standard and originate a FLOGI with this value in the S_ID.

evidence_refs.quote: If the reason code is "Invalid S_ID", the
Nx_Port may originate a FLOGI with a different S_ID:
A) If the S_ID of the rejected FLOGI was 000000h or 0000h || YY, the Nx_Port may select a 24
bit value, XXXXXX, for its N_Port_ID by a method outside this standard and originate a
FLOGI with this value in the S_ID.

text: For the F_RJT response to FLOGI, if the reason code is "Invalid S_ID" and the rejected FLOGI S_ID was XXXXXX, the Nx_Port may select '00 00 00', '00 00 yy', or a new value 'XX XX XX' for its N_Port_ID by a method outside this standard and originate a FLOGI with this value in the S_ID.

evidence_refs.quote: B) If the S_ID of the rejected FLOGI was XXXXXX, the Nx_Port may select a value of '00 00
00' or '00 00 yy', or a new value 'XX XX XX' for its N_Port_ID by a method outside this stan-
dard and originate a FLOGI with this value in the S_ID.

text: A possible FLOGI response is a P_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: P_RJT Sequence, with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

text: For the P_RJT response to FLOGI, the D_ID shall be the S_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: The D_ID shall be the S_ID
of the FLOGI.

text: For the P_RJT response to FLOGI, if the reason code is "Class not supported", the Nx_Port may proceed to N_Port Login in a different class than used for the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: of the FLOGI.  This indicates a point-to-point connection with another Nx_Port.  The reason
code contained in the Payload determines the Nx_Port’s action.  If the reason code is "Class
not supported", the Nx_Port may proceed to N_Port Login in a different class than used for the
FLOGI.

text: For the P_RJT response to FLOGI, for reason codes other than "Class not supported", the Nx_Port should respond accordingly.

evidence_refs.quote: FLOGI.  For other reason codes, the Nx_Port should respond accordingly.

text: A possible FLOGI response is an LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: g) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.

text: For the LS_RJT response to FLOGI, the D_ID of the LS_RJT shall be the N_Port_ID assigned by the Fabric.

evidence_refs.quote: The D_ID of the LS_RJT
shall be the N_Port_ID assigned by the Fabric.

text: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was 000000h, then for the LS_RJT response the D_ID shall be XXXXXX.

evidence_refs.quote: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was 000000h, the
D_ID shall be XXXXXX.

text: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was 0000h || YY, then for the LS_RJT response the D_ID shall be XXXXYY.

evidence_refs.quote: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was 0000h || YY, the D_ID shall be XXXXYY.

text: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was XXXXXX, then for the LS_RJT response the D_ID shall be XXXXXX.

evidence_refs.quote: If the S_ID in the FLOGI was XXXXXX, the D_ID shall be XXXXXX.

text: For the LS_RJT response to FLOGI, the reason code contained in the payload determines the Nx_Port's action, and the Nx_Port may alter the Service Parameters based on the reason code and originate a new FLOGI.

evidence_refs.quote: The reason code con-
tained in the Payload determines the Nx_Port’s action.  The Nx_Port may alter the Service Pa-
rameters based on the reason code and originate a new FLOGI.

text: No Response to FLOGI may indicate a delivery error such as an error on the physical transport.

evidence_refs.quote: h) No Response may indicate a delivery error, e.g., error on the physical transport.  The Nx_Port

text: If there is no response to FLOGI, the Nx_Port shall perform error recovery per FC-FS-2 and may originate a new FLOGI after recovery.

evidence_refs.quote: shall perform error recovery per FC-FS-2.  The Nx_Port may originate a new FLOGI after re-
covery.

text: If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name, then the Nx_Port is connected to itself, this case is outside the scope of the standard, and the FLOGI is discarded.

evidence_refs.quote: If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name, then the Nx_Port is connected to it-
self and this case is outside the scope of this standard.  The FLOGI is discarded.
